creator: mikeg@bsd-box.net
text:
<poke poke poke> Still seems to exist in 10.1-RELEASE :-)

It looks like bsdinstall tries to do the right thing, but fails: when I create more than one slice the *last* slice is set as active (and since / is on the first slice that breaks the first boot unless you drop to a shell and manually set the first slice as active).

Easy to reproduce: Install with MBR partitions & create >1 slices & you'll get results similar to the attachment.

The ideal fix would be making it possible to manually specify which partition should be active, but a quick fix would be to always set the *first* slice as active which is probably The Right Thing most of the time.
